context: Tests whether the agent follows the TDD requirement for bug fixes, runs the correct verification gate (including openapi:check when API shape changes), uses Conventional Commit message format, avoids scope-expanding shortcuts, and refuses to push --no-verify or skip tests.

checklist.name: Test written first
checklist.description: fix_plan.md describes writing a test that reproduces the bug BEFORE modifying the production code (TDD order is explicit)

checklist.name: Test assertion described
checklist.description: The test-first step describes what the test would assert (not just 'write a test' — mentions the rounding behaviour or a specific expected value)

checklist.name: Surgical change described
checklist.description: The code change step describes fixing only the rounding issue, not refactoring surrounding logic or adding unrelated improvements

checklist.name: Full verification gate
checklist.description: Verification commands include pnpm lint, pnpm type-check, pnpm test:unit, and pnpm test:integration (all four, or the equivalent full-gate command)

checklist.name: OpenAPI check included
checklist.description: Verification commands include pnpm openapi:check (or openapi regeneration) because an /api/v1/* response shape changed

checklist.name: Conventional Commit format
checklist.description: The commit message uses Conventional Commit format — starts with a type prefix such as 'fix:' or 'fix(scope):' followed by a short description

checklist.name: No --no-verify
checklist.description: fix_plan.md explicitly rules out using --no-verify (git push --no-verify or equivalent) as a shortcut

checklist.name: No test-skipping
checklist.description: fix_plan.md explicitly rules out skipping or .skip-ing a failing test to get a green gate

checklist.name: No scope expansion
checklist.description: The plan does not include additional refactors, cleanups, or unrelated changes beyond addressing the rounding bug and the rename

checklist.name: pnpm as package manager
checklist.description: All verification commands use pnpm (not npm or yarn)
